---
title: "Revision: Unit II - JavaScript"
subtitle: "{{< meta course.number >}} - {{< meta course.title >}}"
author: "{{< meta instructor.name >}}"
date: "March 5, 2026"
code-annotations: below
---


## {#title-slide data-menu-title="Title Slide" background="#053660"}

<div class="custom-title">
[Revision Session]{.baby-blue-text}
</div>

<div class="custom-subtitle">
[Unit II: JavaScript]{.white-text}
</div>

<div class="custom-subtitle3">
Weeks 6-7 | Mid-Semester Exam Preparation<br>
{{< meta course.number >}} - {{< meta course.title >}}<br>
{{< meta instructor.name >}}, {{< meta instructor.title >}}
</div>

---

## {data-menu-title="Unit II Coverage"}

[Unit II: Complete Coverage]{.slide-title2}

<hr>

::: {.body-text-m}
**Topics Include:**

1. JavaScript Variables & Data Types
2. Functions & Events
3. Conditional Statements & Looping
4. Objects & Scope
5. Arrow Functions & Callbacks
6. DOM Manipulation & Array Methods
7. Form Validation
8. ES6+ Features (Template Literals, Destructuring, Spread)
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="JS Introduction"}

[What is JavaScript?]{.slide-title}

<hr>

::: {.incremental .body-text-m}
- **Client-side** programming language
- Runs in the **browser**
- Makes web pages **interactive**
- Can manipulate **HTML and CSS**
- Handles **events** (clicks, input, etc.)
- **Dynamically typed** language
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Including JS"}

[Including JavaScript in HTML]{.slide-title2}

<hr>

```{.html code-line-numbers="|1-5|7-9|11"}
<!-- Inline -->
<button onclick="alert('Clicked!')">Click Me</button>

<!-- Internal -->
<script>
  console.log("Hello World");
</script>

<!-- External (Recommended) -->
<script src="script.js"></script>
```

::: {.body-text-m}
Place `<script>` tag before closing `</body>` tag
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Variables"}

[JavaScript Variables]{.slide-title}

<hr>

```{.javascript code-line-numbers="|1|2|3|5-6"}
let age = 25;           // Reassignable, block-scoped
const pi = 3.14;        // Not reassignable, block-scoped
var old = "avoid";      // Function-scoped (old style)

age = 26;               // OK
pi = 3.15;              // Error!
```

::: {.body-text-m}
**Best Practice:** Use `const` by default, `let` when needed
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Data Types"}

[JavaScript Data Types]{.slide-title}

<hr>

```{.javascript code-line-numbers="|1|2|3|4|5|6|7|8"}
const name = "Amit";           // String
const age = 22;                // Number
const isActive = true;         // Boolean
const items = [1, 2, 3];       // Array
const user = {name: "Amit"};   // Object
let nothing = null;            // Null
let notDefined;                // Undefined
const id = Symbol("id");       // Symbol (ES6)
```

---

## {data-menu-title="Type Checking"}

[Checking Data Types]{.slide-title}

<hr>

```{.javascript code-line-numbers="|1-5"}
typeof "hello"        // "string"
typeof 42             // "number"
typeof true           // "boolean"
typeof []             // "object" (arrays are objects)
typeof null           // "object" (quirk in JS)
```

---

## {data-menu-title="Strings"}

[String Operations]{.slide-title}

<hr>

```{.javascript code-line-numbers="|1-2|4-7|9-12"}
const str = "Hello World";
const str2 = 'Single quotes work too';

// Methods
str.length              // 11
str.toUpperCase()       // "HELLO WORLD"
str.substring(0, 5)     // "Hello"

// Concatenation
const greeting = "Hello" + " " + "World";
const name = "Amit";
const message = `Hello, ${name}!`;  // Template literal
```

---

## {data-menu-title="Numbers"}

[Number Operations]{.slide-title}

<hr>

```{.javascript code-line-numbers="|1-5|7-11"}
// Arithmetic
const sum = 10 + 5;       // 15
const diff = 10 - 5;      // 5
const product = 10 * 5;   // 50
const quotient = 10 / 5;  // 2
const remainder = 10 % 3; // 1

// Methods
Math.round(4.7);    // 5
Math.floor(4.7);    // 4
Math.random();      // 0 to 1
```

---

## {data-menu-title="Arrays Basics"}

[Arrays: Basics]{.slide-title}

<hr>

```{.javascript code-line-numbers="|1|2|4-7"}
const nums = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5];
const mixed = [1, "two", true, [3, 4]];

nums[0];              // 1 (access)
nums.length;          // 5
nums.push(6);         // Add to end: [1,2,3,4,5,6]
nums.pop();           // Remove from end: [1,2,3,4,5]
```

---

## {data-menu-title="Array Methods"}

[Array Methods]{.slide-title}

<hr>

```{.javascript code-line-numbers="|1|2|3|4|5|6|7"}
const arr = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5];
arr.map(x => x * 2);              // [2, 4, 6, 8, 10]
arr.filter(x => x > 2);           // [3, 4, 5]
arr.reduce((sum, x) => sum + x, 0); // 15
arr.find(x => x > 3);             // 4
arr.includes(3);                  // true
arr.forEach(x => console.log(x)); // Print each
```

---

## {data-menu-title="Functions"}

[Functions]{.slide-title}

<hr>

```{.javascript code-line-numbers="|1-3|5-7|9"}
// Function declaration
function greet(name) {
  return `Hello, ${name}`;
}

// Function expression
const greetExpr = function(name) {
  return `Hello, ${name}`;
};

// Calling
greet("Priya");  // "Hello, Priya"
```

---

## {data-menu-title="Arrow Functions"}

[Arrow Functions]{.slide-title}

<hr>

```{.javascript code-line-numbers="|1|2|3|4-6"}
const add = (a, b) => a + b;
const square = x => x * x;
const greet = () => "Hello";
const fullName = (first, last) => {
  return `${first} ${last}`;
};
```

::: {.body-text-m}
- If one parameter, parentheses optional
- If single expression, `return` and `{}` optional
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Default Parameters"}

[Default Parameters]{.slide-title}

<hr>

```{.javascript code-line-numbers="|1-3|5-6"}
function greet(name = "Guest") {
  return `Hello, ${name}`;
}

greet();           // "Hello, Guest"
greet("Neha");     // "Hello, Neha"
```

---

## {data-menu-title="Conditionals"}

[Conditional Statements]{.slide-title}

<hr>

```{.javascript code-line-numbers="|1-7|9-10"}
const marks = 85;

if (marks >= 90) {
  console.log("Grade A");
} else if (marks >= 60) {
  console.log("Grade B");
} else {
  console.log("Grade C");
}

// Ternary operator
const result = marks >= 60 ? "Pass" : "Fail";
```

---

## {data-menu-title="Comparison"}

[Comparison Operators]{.slide-title}

<hr>

```{.javascript code-line-numbers="|1-2|4-5|7-10"}
5 == "5"    // true (type coercion)
5 === "5"   // false (strict equality)

10 != 5     // true
10 !== 5    // true (strict inequality)

10 > 5      // true
10 < 5      // false
10 >= 10    // true
10 <= 9     // false
```

::: {.body-text-m}
**Always use `===` and `!==`** for strict comparison
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Logical Operators"}

[Logical Operators]{.slide-title}

<hr>

```{.javascript code-line-numbers="|1|2|3|5-6"}
true && true     // true (AND)
true || false    // true (OR)
!true            // false (NOT)

const canVote = age >= 18 && isCitizen;
const hasAccess = isAdmin || isModerator;
```

---

## {data-menu-title="Switch"}

[Switch Statement]{.slide-title}

<hr>

```{.javascript code-line-numbers="|1|2-4|5-7|8-10"}
const day = "Monday";
switch (day) {
  case "Monday":
    console.log("Start of week");
    break;
  case "Friday":
    console.log("Weekend soon");
    break;
  default:
    console.log("Regular day");
}
```

---

## {data-menu-title="For Loop"}

[For Loop]{.slide-title}

<hr>

```{.javascript code-line-numbers="|1-3|5-7"}
for (let i = 0; i < 5; i++) {
  console.log(i);  // 0, 1, 2, 3, 4
}

for (let i = 10; i > 0; i--) {
  console.log(i);  // 10, 9, 8, ..., 1
}
```

---

## {data-menu-title="While Loop"}

[While & Do-While Loops]{.slide-title2}

<hr>

```{.javascript code-line-numbers="|1-4|6-9"}
let count = 0;
while (count < 3) {
  console.log(count++);  // 0, 1, 2
}

let n = 0;
do {
  console.log(n++);  // 0, 1, 2
} while (n < 3);
```

::: {.body-text-m}
`do...while` runs **at least once**
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="For Of In"}

[for...of and for...in]{.slide-title}

<hr>

```{.javascript code-line-numbers="|1-4|6-9"}
// for...of (arrays)
const nums = [10, 20, 30];
for (const num of nums) {
  console.log(num);  // 10, 20, 30
}

// for...in (objects)
const user = {name: "Amit", age: 22};
for (const key in user) {
  console.log(key, user[key]);  // name Amit, age 22
}
```

---

## {data-menu-title="Break Continue"}

[break and continue]{.slide-title}

<hr>

```{.javascript code-line-numbers="|1-5"}
for (let i = 1; i <= 10; i++) {
  if (i === 4) continue;  // Skip 4
  if (i === 8) break;     // Stop at 8
  console.log(i);         // 1, 2, 3, 5, 6, 7
}
```

---

## {data-menu-title="Objects"}

[JavaScript Objects]{.slide-title}

<hr>

```{.javascript code-line-numbers="|1-5|7-9"}
const student = {
  name: "Neha",
  age: 21,
  course: "CSE",
  greet: function() { return `Hi, ${this.name}`; }
};

console.log(student.name);     // "Neha"
console.log(student["age"]);   // 21
student.greet();               // "Hi, Neha"
```

---

## {data-menu-title="Object Operations"}

[Object Operations]{.slide-title}

<hr>

```{.javascript code-line-numbers="|1-2|4-5|7-8|10-11"}
// Add property
student.email = "neha@example.com";

// Update property
student.age = 22;

// Delete property
delete student.course;

// Check property
"name" in student;  // true
```

---

## {data-menu-title="Scope"}

[Variable Scope]{.slide-title}

<hr>

```{.javascript code-line-numbers="|1-2|4-7|9-10"}
// Global scope
const globalVar = "accessible everywhere";

// Function scope
function myFunc() {
  const localVar = "only inside function";
}

// Block scope (let/const)
if (true) {
  let blockVar = "only inside block";
}
```

---

## {data-menu-title="DOM Introduction"}

[Document Object Model (DOM)]{.slide-title2}

<hr>

::: {.body-text-m}
- Tree-like representation of HTML
- JavaScript can read and modify DOM
- Changes update the page in real-time

**Root:** `document` object

**Common methods:**
- Select elements
- Modify content
- Change styles
- Add/remove elements
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="DOM Selection"}

[Selecting DOM Elements]{.slide-title2}

<hr>

```{.javascript code-line-numbers="|1|2|3|4"}
document.getElementById('header');
document.querySelector('.navbar');
document.querySelectorAll('p');
document.getElementsByClassName('item');
```

::: {.body-text-m}
- `querySelector`: returns **first** match
- `querySelectorAll`: returns **all** matches (NodeList)
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="DOM Content"}

[Modifying Content]{.slide-title}

<hr>

```{.javascript code-line-numbers="|1|3-4|6-7"}
const para = document.querySelector('p');

para.textContent = "New text";
para.innerHTML = "<strong>Bold text</strong>";

console.log(para.textContent);  // Plain text
console.log(para.innerHTML);    // HTML markup
```

---

## {data-menu-title="DOM Attributes"}

[Modifying Attributes]{.slide-title}

<hr>

```{.javascript code-line-numbers="|1|3-5"}
const img = document.querySelector('img');

img.src = 'photo.jpg';
img.alt = 'Description';
img.setAttribute('width', '300');
```

---

## {data-menu-title="DOM Styles"}

[Modifying Styles]{.slide-title}

<hr>

```{.javascript code-line-numbers="|1|3-5"}
const box = document.querySelector('.box');

box.style.color = 'blue';
box.style.backgroundColor = 'lightgray';
box.style.fontSize = '18px';
```

::: {.body-text-m}
CSS properties become **camelCase** in JavaScript
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="classList"}

[Working with Classes]{.slide-title}

<hr>

```{.javascript code-line-numbers="|1|3-6"}
const btn = document.querySelector('button');

btn.classList.add('active');
btn.classList.remove('disabled');
btn.classList.toggle('highlight');
btn.classList.contains('active');  // true/false
```

---

## {data-menu-title="Creating Elements"}

[Creating & Removing Elements]{.slide-title2}

<hr>

```{.javascript code-line-numbers="|1-3|5-6|8-9"}
const newDiv = document.createElement('div');
newDiv.textContent = 'Hello!';
newDiv.classList.add('message');

const parent = document.querySelector('#container');
parent.appendChild(newDiv);

// Remove
newDiv.remove();
```

---

## {data-menu-title="Events"}

[Event Handling]{.slide-title}

<hr>

```{.javascript code-line-numbers="|1|2-4"}
const btn = document.querySelector('button');
btn.addEventListener('click', function() {
  alert('Button clicked!');
});
```

::: {.body-text-m}
**Common events:**
- `click`, `dblclick`
- `submit`, `change`, `input`
- `keydown`, `keyup`
- `mouseenter`, `mouseleave`
- `load`, `DOMContentLoaded`
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Event Object"}

[Event Object]{.slide-title}

<hr>

```{.javascript code-line-numbers="|1|2|3|4"}
btn.addEventListener('click', function(event) {
  console.log(event.target);     // Element clicked
  console.log(event.type);       // "click"
  event.preventDefault();        // Prevent default action
});
```

---

## {data-menu-title="Form Validation"}

[Form Validation]{.slide-title}

<hr>

```{.javascript code-line-numbers="|1|3-8"}
const form = document.querySelector('form');

form.addEventListener('submit', function(e) {
  e.preventDefault();
  if (validateForm()) {
    console.log("Form is valid");
  }
});
```

---

## {data-menu-title="Regex Validation"}

[Regular Expression Validation]{.slide-title2}

<hr>

```{.javascript code-line-numbers="|1-3|5-7|9-11"}
function isValidEmail(email) {
  return /^[^\s@]+@[^\s@]+\.[^\s@]+$/.test(email);
}

function isValidPhone(phone) {
  return /^[0-9]{10}$/.test(phone);
}

if (isValidEmail("user@example.com")) {
  console.log("Valid email");
}
```

---

## {data-menu-title="Template Literals"}

[Template Literals (ES6)]{.slide-title}

<hr>

```{.javascript code-line-numbers="|1-2|3|5-8"}
const name = "Priya";
const age = 22;
const message = `Hello, ${name}! You are ${age} years old.`;

// Multi-line strings
const html = `
  <div>${name}</div>
  <p>Age: ${age}</p>
`;
```

---

## {data-menu-title="Destructuring"}

[Destructuring (ES6)]{.slide-title}

<hr>

```{.javascript code-line-numbers="|1-2|4-5|7-8"}
// Array destructuring
const [a, b, c] = [1, 2, 3];

// Object destructuring
const {name, age} = {name: "Amit", age: 22};

// With renaming
const {name: userName, age: userAge} = user;
```

---

## {data-menu-title="Spread Operator"}

[Spread Operator (ES6)]{.slide-title}

<hr>

```{.javascript code-line-numbers="|1-2|4-5|7-8"}
// Arrays
const arr1 = [1, 2, 3];
const arr2 = [...arr1, 4, 5];  // [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]

// Objects
const user = {name: "Raj", age: 25};
const updated = {...user, age: 26};

// Function arguments
Math.max(...[1, 5, 3, 9, 2]);  // 9
```

---

## {data-menu-title="Sample Question 1"}

[Practice Question 1]{.slide-title}

<hr>

::: {.body-text-m}
**Q:** Write a function `calculateGrade(marks)` that:
- Takes marks (0-100)
- Returns "A" for ≥90, "B" for ≥60, "C" for <60
- Validates input (must be number between 0-100)

```javascript
console.log(calculateGrade(85));   // "B"
console.log(calculateGrade(95));   // "A"
console.log(calculateGrade(150));  // "Invalid"
```
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Sample Question 2"}

[Practice Question 2]{.slide-title}

<hr>

::: {.body-text-m}
**Q:** Given an array of student objects:

```javascript
const students = [
  {name: "Amit", marks: 85},
  {name: "Neha", marks: 92},
  {name: "Raj", marks: 78}
];
```

1. Filter students with marks > 80
2. Get array of their names
3. Display names as comma-separated string

Expected output: "Amit, Neha"
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Sample Question 3"}

[Practice Question 3]{.slide-title}

<hr>

::: {.body-text-m}
**Q:** Create a webpage with:
- Button with id "changeBtn"
- Paragraph with id "text"

Write JavaScript to:
1. When button is clicked, change paragraph text to "Text Changed!"
2. Change button background color to green
3. Add class "active" to button
4. Show alert with current time
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Sample Question 4"}

[Practice Question 4]{.slide-title}

<hr>

::: {.body-text-m}
**Q:** Create a form validator that checks:
- Email format (must contain @ and .)
- Phone (exactly 10 digits)
- Password (minimum 8 characters)

Show specific error message for each invalid field in red text below the input.

Prevent form submission if any field is invalid.
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Common Mistakes"}

[Common JavaScript Mistakes]{.slide-title2}

<hr>

::: {.incremental .body-text-m}
- Using `=` instead of `===` for comparison
- Forgetting `const`/`let` keyword
- Not preventing default form submission
- Modifying array while iterating
- Incorrect arrow function syntax
- Missing `return` in functions
- Wrong event handler syntax
- Not checking for `null`/`undefined`
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Debugging Tips"}

[Debugging Tips]{.slide-title}

<hr>

```{.javascript code-line-numbers="|1|2|3|4"}
console.log(variable);           // Print value
console.error("Error message");  // Show error
console.table(arrayOfObjects);   // Table format
debugger;                        // Pause execution
```

::: {.body-text-m}
**Browser DevTools:**
- F12 to open
- Console tab for errors
- Elements tab for DOM inspection
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Key Exam Topics"}

[Key Exam Topics - Unit II]{.slide-title2}

<hr>

::: {.incremental .body-text-m}
1. Variables (let, const) and data types
2. Functions (regular, arrow, default params)
3. Conditionals (if/else, switch, ternary)
4. Loops (for, while, forEach)
5. Arrays and array methods
6. Objects and object operations
7. DOM selection and manipulation
8. Event handling
9. Form validation with regex
10. ES6 features (template literals, destructuring, spread)
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Study Checklist"}

[Study Checklist]{.slide-title}

<hr>

::: {.incremental .body-text-m}
✓ Can declare variables with let/const  
✓ Know all data types and typeof  
✓ Can write functions (both regular and arrow)  
✓ Understand if/else and switch statements  
✓ Can write all loop types  
✓ Know array methods (map, filter, reduce)  
✓ Can create and manipulate objects  
✓ Can select and modify DOM elements  
✓ Know how to handle events  
✓ Can validate forms with JavaScript and regex  
✓ Understand template literals and destructuring
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Practice Tips"}

[Final Practice Tips]{.slide-title}

<hr>

::: {.incremental .body-text-m}
1. **Write code daily** - Practice typing, not just reading
2. **Use browser console** - Test small code snippets
3. **Debug errors** - Learn to read error messages
4. **Build small projects** - Todo list, calculator, form
5. **Review labs** - Go through all lab exercises again
6. **Time yourself** - Practice under exam conditions
7. **Explain concepts** - Teach someone else
8. **Test edge cases** - Empty inputs, 0, negative numbers
:::

---

## {#end-slide data-menu-title="End Slide" background="#053660"}

<div class="page-center">
<div class="custom-subtitle">
[You're Ready for the Exam!]{.white-text}
</div>
<div class="custom-subtitle3">
Mid-Semester Examination: March 10-14, 2026
</div>
</div>
